interactions.summary: Interviewer and guest exchange closing remarks, with the guest offering conditional certainty ('I hope I'm wrong') and practical emotional resilience guidance.

speaker_notes.note: Interviewer references prior missed predictions as historical analogy; no factual claim about this source beyond tone framing.

claims.claim: Jiang signals epistemic humility while reiterating that evaluating worst-case pathways remains practical even without perfect confidence.
The interview concludes by emphasizing preparedness and mindset adjustment rather than technical certainty as the key response.

signature_moments.source_phrase: I hope I'm an idiot, okay?
signature_moments.why_it_matters: Explicitly signals uncertainty under high-stakes forecasting while preserving preparedness logic.
Marks source boundary and transition to post-interview editorial cleanup.

glossary_terms.definition_or_usage: Jiang’s practical method: keep attention on scenario preparation rather than defensive certainty when evidence is contested.
Used by interviewer as shorthand for persistent conflict with no clear settlement endpoint.

chronology_notes.note: Source closes with emotional and practical closure, preparing transition to outro and no longer advancing substantive new claims.

uncertainty_notes.note: Outro lines are closing courtesy and do not add durable analytical claims.
